title: The Five Orange Pips
synopsis: Young John Openshaw consults Holmes in a storm-battered evening, reporting that members of his family have died shortly after receiving envelopes containing five orange pips and initials K.K.K. Holmes deduces the threat comes from the Ku Klux Klan, whose papers Openshaw's uncle had brought back from America.
solution: The Ku Klux Klan sent the death warnings demanding return of certain incriminating papers. Despite Holmes's warning, Openshaw is murdered before Holmes can protect him. Holmes sends his own message to the Klan's ship captain, and learns later that the vessel sank in the Atlantic — Holmes regards this as justice delivered by fate.
notable_elements: One of the rare cases where Holmes fails to save the client
The Ku Klux Klan as villains — an unusually political story
Holmes's personal fury at Openshaw's murder
Tragic ending with no formal legal resolution
